formats.format: $syslog_priority{name=priority}$ $unix_timestamp{name=timestamp}$ $hostname{name=device_hostname}$ vpn_firewall src=$ip_address{name=src_ip}$ dst=$ip_address{name=dst_ip}$ protocol=$protocol$ sport=$port_number{name=src_port}$ dport=$port_number{name=dst_port}$ pattern: $pattern_action{name=pattern}$

$syslog_priority{name=priority}$ $unix_timestamp{name=timestamp}$ $hostname{name=device_hostname}$ ip_flow src=$ip_address{name=src_ip}$ dst=$ip_address{name=dst_ip}$ protocol=$protocol$ sport=$port_number{name=src_port}$ dport=$port_number{name=dst_port}$ translated_src_ip=$ip_address{name=translated_src_ip}$ translated_port=$port_number{name=translated_port}$

$syslog_priority{name=priority}$ $unix_timestamp{name=timestamp}$ $hostname{name=device_hostname}$ flows $connection_status$ src=$ip_address{name=src_ip}$ dst=$ip_address{name=dst_ip}$ mac=$mac_address{name=src_mac}$ protocol=$protocol$ sport=$port_number{name=src_port}$ dport=$port_number{name=dst_port}$

metadata_details.purpose: Parser for Cisco Meraki flow logs using the OCSF mapping to normalise events into the AI-SIEM schema.
metadata_details.dependency_summary: Requires ingestion of the original Cisco Meraki flow logs.
metadata_details.performance_impact: Minimal – this parser is a placeholder pointing to the upstream definition.
metadata_details.tags: cisco, meraki flow, logs, ocsf, parser
metadata_details.author: Joel Mora

analysis.semantic_summary: This parser processes Cisco Meraki network flow logs, handling three distinct formats: VPN firewall traffic, IP flow data with NAT translations, and general network flows. It normalizes network traffic data including source/destination IPs, ports, protocols, and connection states into a standardized OCSF schema for security monitoring and analysis.
optimization_opportunities: Consolidate duplicate rewrite rules across formats
Add validation for IP address formats
Implement more specific pattern matching for connection states
Add error handling for malformed syslog messages
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: cisco_meraki_flow_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Network Activity (4001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:23:23.329132

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local PATTERNS = {
    ip = "^%d+%.%d+%.%d+%.%d+$",
    port = "^%d+$",
    protocol = "^(tcp|udp|icmp)$",
    mac = "^%x%x:%x%x:%x%x:%x%x:%x%x:%x%x$"
}

-- Cached reference for performance
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format
local os_time = os.time

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize output structure with nested tables
    local output = {
        -- OCSF classification 
        class_uid = 4001,
        class_name = "Network Activity",
        category_uid = 4,
        category_name = "Network Activity",
        type_uid = 400101,
        activity_id = 1,
        activity_name = "Traffic",

        -- Initialize nested structures
        src_endpoint = {},
        dst_endpoint = {},
        connection_info = {},
        device = {},
        metadata = {}
    }

    -- Optimized field transformations using local references
    local src_endpoint = output.src_endpoint
    local dst_endpoint = output.dst_endpoint
    local connection_info = output.connection_info
    local device = output.device

    -- Timestamp handling with validation
    if record.timestamp then
        local ts = tonumber(record.timestamp)
        if ts then
            output.time = ts
        end
    end

    -- IP address transformations with validation
    if record.src_ip and string_match(record.src_ip, PATTERNS.ip) then
        src_endpoint.ip = record.src_ip
    end
    
    if record.dst_ip and string_match(record.dst_ip, PATTERNS.ip) then
        dst_endpoint.ip = record.dst_ip
    end

    -- Port handling with validation
    if record.src_port and string_match(record.src_port, PATTERNS.port) then
        src_endpoint.port = tonumber(record.src_port)
    end

    if record.dst_port and string_match(record.dst_port, PATTERNS.port) then
        dst_endpoint.port = tonumber(record.dst_port)
    end

    -- Protocol handling
    if record.protocol and string_match(record.protocol, PATTERNS.protocol) then
        connection_info.protocol_name = record.protocol
    end

    -- Device hostname
    if record.device_hostname then
        device.hostname = record.device_hostname
    end

    -- MAC address handling with validation
    if record.src_mac and string_match(record.src_mac, PATTERNS.mac) then
        src_endpoint.mac = record.src_mac
    end

    -- Connection status mapping
    if record.connection_status then
        connection_info.boundary = record.connection_status
        -- Map status_id based on connection state
        output.status_id = ({
            start = 1,
            allowed = 1,
            tear = 2
        })[record.connection_status] or 0
    end

    -- Handle translated IPs for NAT
    if record.translated_src_ip and string_match(record.translated_src_ip, PATTERNS.ip) then
        dst_endpoint.intermediate_ips = {record.translated_src_ip}
    end

    -- Validation and cleanup
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Generate observables for correlation
    if src_endpoint.ip and dst_endpoint.ip then
        output.observables = string_format(
            '[{"name":"src_ip","type":"IP Address","value":"%s"},{"name":"dst_ip","type":"IP Address","value":"%s"}]',
            src_endpoint.ip,
            dst_endpoint.ip
        )
    end

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

test_cases.expected: Complete OCSF output with networking fields
